Politics.headline: Starmer hopes to regain momentum with unveiling of dozens of bills in king's speech
Politics.detail: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is attempting to regain political momentum by unveiling a series of new bills during the King's Speech, amid challenges to his leadership.

Politics.headline: Trump issues new threat to Iran before leaving for China
Politics.detail: Before departing for China, former U.S. President Donald Trump issued a new threat to Iran, amidst ongoing tensions and conflict in the region.

Politics.headline: Kash Patel lashes out as lawmakers question 'excessive drinking'
Politics.detail: FBI Director Kash Patel testified during a Senate hearing and responded strongly to allegations of excessive drinking and being unreachable to his staff.

Politics.headline: Minister Champagne in Québec City to discuss the Spring Economic Update 2026
Politics.detail: The Honourable François-Philippe Champagne, Minister of Finance and National Revenue, will discuss the government's plan to face global uncertainty with fiscal responsibility and pragmatic economic choices, as outlined in the Spring Economic Update 2026.

Politics.headline: Key Republican faces Democratic calls to resign over radio interview
Politics.detail: Rep. Jen Kiggans (R-Va.) is facing calls to resign from Democratic colleagues who say she expressed agreement with a radio host's remark about House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries. Kiggans denies agreeing with the language.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Inflation Jumps to 3.8% as Iran War Drives Up Prices
Finance & Economy.detail: The annual inflation rate in the US rose to 3.8% in April, the highest since May 2023, as the war with Iran continues to push energy prices higher; the core inflation rate also hit a 7-month high.

Finance & Economy.headline: Japan Current Account Surplus Hits Record High
Finance & Economy.detail: Japan's current account surplus increased to JPY 4,681.5 billion in March 2026, marking the largest amount on record, driven by a surge in the goods account surplus as export growth outpaced imports.

Finance & Economy.headline: Gold Pressured by Strong US Inflation Data
Finance & Economy.detail: Gold traded near $4,700 an ounce, facing downward pressure after stronger-than-expected US inflation figures reduced expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts, with investors anticipating unchanged interest rates for the remainder of the year.

Finance & Economy.headline: Americans Lean on Credit Cards as Gas Prices Surge
Finance & Economy.detail: Rising gas prices are pushing lower-income consumers to rely more on credit cards and buy now, pay later services, as fuel costs climb to their highest share of household income since March 2022.

Finance & Economy.headline: More Canadians are filing for insolvency
Finance & Economy.detail: More Canadians are filing for insolvency according to the latest data from the Office of the Superintendent of Bankruptcy, as rising costs stretch consumers to their limits.

Technology.headline: Google Discovers First AI-Made Zero-Day Exploit, Claims It Was Not Made With Gemini
Technology.detail: Google has discovered the first instance of a zero-day exploit created by artificial intelligence, assuring the public that its Gemini AI was not involved in the exploit's creation. The discovery highlights both the capabilities and potential risks associated with AI in cybersecurity.

Technology.headline: Apple May Keep iPhone 18 Prices Stable Despite Rising Memory Costs
Technology.detail: Despite increasing costs for memory components, Apple is reportedly planning to maintain stable pricing for the upcoming iPhone 18. This decision may be influenced by market competition and a desire to maintain consumer demand.

Technology.headline: New 'Googlebook' Laptop Category Puts AI Front and Center
Technology.detail: Google is introducing a new category of laptops called 'Googlebook,' which will heavily integrate artificial intelligence functionalities. This initiative signals a push to combine Android and Chrome OS into a unified platform.

Technology.headline: iOS 26.5 Is Here With End-to-End Encrypted RCS
Technology.detail: Apple's iOS 26.5 update introduces end-to-end encryption for RCS messaging, enhancing the security and privacy of communications on iPhones. The update also includes other new features and improvements.

Technology.headline: Amazon Expands 30-Minutes Deliveries to These US Cities
Technology.detail: Amazon is expanding its 'Amazon Now' service, offering 30-minute deliveries to numerous cities across the United States, aiming to reach tens of millions of customers by the end of the year. This expansion builds upon initial tests in Seattle and Philadelphia.

Science.headline: Engineered exosomes reverse sleep deprivation brain damage in mice
Science.detail: New research shows that engineered exosomes can reverse brain damage caused by sleep deprivation in mice, offering potential therapeutic avenues for sleep disorders and related cognitive impairments.

Science.headline: Work songs can improve team coordination, study finds
Science.detail: A recent study has found that singing work songs together can enhance team coordination and improve efficiency in collaborative tasks.

Science.headline: Before and After the 2025 Tsunami in Alaska
Science.detail: In 2025, a retreating glacier in Alaska caused a landslide into a fjord named Tracy Arm which triggered a tsunami that swept down the fjord into the ocean and reached a height of more than 480 meters, the second highest tsunami ever recorded.

Science.headline: 80 years after the Trinity nuclear test, scientists identify new molecule-trapping crystal formed in the blast.
Science.detail: Scientists have identified a new type of crystal formed during the Trinity nuclear test 80 years ago, which has the ability to trap molecules.

Science.headline: Territorial conflict may explain male primates' large size
Science.detail: Male primates may be larger than females partly because of pressure from rival groups, not just competition with males inside their own group, suggesting territorial conflicts play a role in primate size.

Health.headline: Scientists Reversed Biological Age in Older Adults with a 4-week Diet Change
Health.detail: A new University of Sydney study found that a four-week diet change was enough to make some older adults appear biologically younger by reducing fat intake or shifting toward more plant-based foods.

Health.headline: The condition PCOS is now called PMOS. What to know about the name change and what it means for care
Health.detail: A hormonal condition affecting 1 in 8 women around the world just got a new name, PMOS (polycystic metabolic ovarian syndrome), in hopes of improving care.

Health.headline: NKF 2026. IgA Nephropathy: Advances Drive a Wave of New Therapies
Health.detail: Advances in understanding IgA nephropathy are leading to a surge of new therapies, offering hope for improved treatment options.

Health.headline: Daily pill can help people maintain weight loss after they come off jabs, trial shows
Health.detail: A daily pill has shown promise in helping individuals maintain their weight loss after discontinuing weight loss injections, according to trial results.

Health.headline: Older people risk mental decline if they do long hours of caring, UK study shows
Health.detail: A UK study indicates that older individuals who spend long hours providing care may face an increased risk of mental decline.

Sports.headline: AFL urged to step in as alarming 'bollard' trend sparks umpire safety concerns
Sports.detail: Ray Chamberlain is urging the AFL to step in and help stop umpire 'bollard' trend amid safety fears, highlighting an on-field trend that has led to some umpire concussions.

Sports.headline: PGA Championship starting times at Aronimink
Sports.detail: The starting times for the PGA Championship at Aronimink have been released.

Sports.headline: Collin Morikawa in search of a 2nd PGA title and hoping his cranky back is up to the task
Sports.detail: Collin Morikawa is seeking his second PGA title, hoping his back will hold up.

Sports.headline: Lens vs Paris 2026-05-13
Sports.detail: Preview of the Lens vs Paris Saint-Germain match.

Sports.headline: Sports News from Wednesday, May 13th, 2026
Sports.detail: Kfm provides sports news for May 13th 2026.

Entertainment.headline: Bonnie Tyler in induced coma after emergency surgery in Portugal
Entertainment.detail: The singer was reportedly placed in an induced coma following emergency surgery in Portugal.

Entertainment.headline: Hayden Panettiere recalls being told to perform sexual acts with 'very famous' man
Entertainment.detail: Hayden Panettiere has shared a shocking revelation about being pressured to perform sexual acts with a 'very famous' man early in her career.

Entertainment.headline: Rod Stewart praises King Charles lll for putting 'ratbag' Trump 'in his place'
Entertainment.detail: Rod Stewart has lauded King Charles III for his handling of Donald Trump.

Entertainment.headline: ‘Dancing With the Stars’ Season 35 Reveals 3rd Celeb Cast Member's Identity
Entertainment.detail: The 35th season of 'Dancing With the Stars' has revealed its third celebrity cast member.

Entertainment.headline: Vijay's blockbuster opening: How cinema powered the superstar's rise in Tamil Nadu politics
Entertainment.detail: This article discusses how cinema has influenced Vijay's rise in Tamil Nadu politics.

Crime & Justice.headline: Charges after Baltimore's Key Bridge collapse
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department announced criminal charges against the ship operator in the Francis Scott Key Bridge collapse that killed six people in 2024.

Crime & Justice.headline: Mayor faces prison time over alleged spying
Crime & Justice.detail: Eileen Wang, the former mayor of Arcadia, California, faces a 10-year prison sentence for pleading guilty to acting as a covert agent for China.

Crime & Justice.headline: New details about Boston-area highway gunman
Crime & Justice.detail: The suspect who allegedly fired into a major road near Boston had prior criminal convictions.

Crime & Justice.headline: 'I'm afraid if she gets out': US author's sons say they want mother to stay in jail
Crime & Justice.detail: Kouri Richins, 35, faces up to life in prison for killing her husband, and her three sons say they are 'afraid' of her.

Crime & Justice.headline: Louisiana police to pay $4.85m to daughter of Black motorist who died at officers' hands in 2019
Crime & Justice.detail: Police and sheriff's office agree to settlement after Ronald Greene was fatally shocked and punched during 2019 arrest.

Education.headline: All 11 teachers accused of creating toxic climate at Montreal school have their licences revoked
Education.detail: All 11 teachers who were suspended in 2024 for allegedly fostering a toxic climate at a Montreal elementary school have had their licenses revoked, according to the office of Quebec's education minister. Confirmation of the 11 licenses being revoked came on the same day the Education Ministry made public a final report into the situation at Bedford elementary school.

Education.headline: 2026 OC AVID senior standouts earn $431,000 in scholarships
Education.detail: A total of $431,000 in scholarships was awarded to 42 graduating seniors during the 2026 OC AVID Senior Standout Recognition and Scholarship Presentation, held April 15 at the Irvine Barclay Theatre. The event honored 57 students for academic achievement, leadership and perseverance.

Education.headline: Latest Canvas Attack Shows Schools Still Struggle With Cybersecurity
Education.detail: A recent cyberattack on Canvas, a widely used learning management system, highlights the ongoing cybersecurity challenges faced by schools. The attack led to disruptions for numerous schools and colleges, raising concerns about data security and the vulnerability of educational institutions to cyber threats.

Education.headline: Pediatrics group issues new guidance on recess for the first time in 13 years
Education.detail: The American Academy of Pediatrics has released updated guidelines on recess, marking the first update in 13 years. The new guidance emphasizes the importance of recess for children's physical and mental health.

Education.headline: Canvas's owner strikes deal with hackers who disrupted thousands of schools.
Education.detail: Instructure, the owner of Canvas, an education platform, dealt with hackers after a data breach that affected almost 9,000 schools. The company has not disclosed whether they paid a ransom for the return of stolen personal data.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: KMT's stance on the CCP
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Chinese Nationalist Party (KMT) and the Taiwan People's Party (TPP) used their legislative majority to push their version of a special defense budget bill to fund the purchase of US military equipment.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Protecting Taiwan's cables at sea
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The US Senate Committee on Foreign Relations has formally elevated damage to undersea cables to a national security issue, pointing to Russia and China as allegedly using tactics with low attribution risks to threaten subsea infrastructure.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Buddhist heritage
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The revival of Buddhist chants at the ancient Dharmarajika Stupa in Taxila after nearly 1,500 years is a welcome development.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Cotton crisis
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Pakistan's cotton economy is facing a crisis that exposes the country's flawed agricultural policies and requires urgent attention.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Spanberger trades Virginia's influence for national popular vote gamble
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger's signing of Virginia's entry into the National Popular Vote Interstate Compact was characterized as a win for democracy, but the editorial board disagrees.

World.headline: Ukraine says Russia launches 200-plus drones in overnight attacks, leaving 6 dead
World.detail: Russia launched over 200 drones in overnight attacks on Ukraine, resulting in six deaths. The attacks are part of the ongoing conflict between the two countries.

World.headline: French woman with hantavirus has severe form of disease, is in 'final stage of supportive care'
World.detail: A French woman infected with hantavirus during the cruise ship outbreak is critically ill and in the final stage of supportive care, receiving treatment with an artificial lung. The woman is among those affected by the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship.

World.headline: Trump travels to meet China's Xi Jinping as US war on Iran looms large
World.detail: President Donald Trump is in Beijing to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping amidst rising tensions and a potential war with Iran. The meeting is expected to address trade and AI.

World.headline: Honduras mayor arrested for masterminding environmentalist's killing
World.detail: The mayor of Honduras has been arrested for allegedly masterminding the killing of an environmentalist. The arrest highlights the ongoing challenges in protecting environmental activists in the region.

World.headline: Iran warns any new U.S. attacks will bring a "bad result," as President Trump acknowledges the ceasefire is faltering and violence flares in Lebanon.
World.detail: Iran has warned that any new attacks by the U.S. will lead to negative consequences, especially as President Trump admits that the ceasefire is weakening, and violence is increasing in Lebanon. Tensions remain high between the U.S. and Iran.

Zimbabwe.headline: No UN reform without Africa: Zimbabwe
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe emphasizes the importance of Africa in the reform of the United Nations, highlighting the need for greater representation and a stronger voice for the continent on the global stage.

Zimbabwe.headline: No end in sight for Harare's water crisis as residents pay more for less
Zimbabwe.detail: Harare residents continue to face a persistent water crisis, experiencing increased costs for diminished water access, exacerbating the challenges faced by the population.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe says monitoring returning health workers after hantavirus exposure
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is closely monitoring health workers returning to the country after potential exposure to hantavirus, ensuring measures are in place to prevent any potential outbreak.

Zimbabwe.headline: Police launch manhunt for suspected drug lords
Zimbabwe.detail: Police in Zimbabwe have launched a manhunt for individuals suspected of being drug lords, intensifying efforts to combat drug trafficking and related criminal activities.

Zimbabwe.headline: Human rights experts demand halt to plans to abolish gender commission
Zimbabwe.detail: Human rights experts are calling for a stop to the plans to abolish the gender commission.
